title: Recommendations
content:
# Recommendations Recommendations are generated from Kelvin SmartApp™ or from third party sources using the Kelvin API endpoint and will either be automatically accepted and implemented or wait for the Operations Engineers decision on the actions to take. Recommendations can be accessed and controlled from many locations. You can see how to view Recommendations from the different areas of the Kelvin UI * [Assets Menu](#assets-menu) * [Applications Menu](../../../kelvin-smartapps-page/how-to/assets/smartapps-details-tab-assets-recommendation-view.md#applications-menu) * [Data Explorer Recommendation Lane](../../../data-explorer-page/how-to/manage-recommendations/recommendation-view.md#data-explorer-lane) ## Assets Menu There are two main locations that you can view Recommendations for Assets. ### Asset-centric Table If you customize the Asset-centric table columns, you can add a filtered Recommendations column. The default Asset-centric table does not show Recommendations in the column. You can read the documentation on how to show Recommendation column here. In the recommendations column you will see two types of options; Option | UI | Description --- | --- | --- **View More** Button | | This means that there is a pending Recommendation. Clicking will show a popup where you can accept/reject the pending Recommendation. **Details** Link | {height=25} | This means that there are NO pending Recommendations. Clicking will show a popup with the last Recommendation details. When you click on the option, you will see the Recommendation appear inline below the Asset. Here you can see the most important information and perform the normal actions like accept/reject.
=== "List Recommendations" The left part of the inline Recommendation is a list of all the Recommendations for that Asset in chronological order. Here you can select which Recommendation to view in the inline section to the right of this list. === "Actions" The top right part of the inline Recommendation shows all the actions that will be taken if the recommendation is accepted. It is possible to have multiple actions in one Recommendation. This will also give you information about the current setting when the Recommendation was created as well as the Recommended new setting. The actions are not just limited to Control Changes but can also be one of the following; Even if a Recommendation is accepted, this does not mean the Actions will also succeed. It just means the Actions are initiated. There may be cases where a Recommendation is accepted and highlighted green, but the Control Change fails, for example due to Guardrail rule violation, and is highlighted Red. * Control Changes * Send Emails and Alerts (using Custom Actions) * Create Alarms (future feature) === "Evidences" The bottom right part of the inline Recommendation shows all the shows the evidences attached to the Recommendation. Evidence are attachments to the recommendation that provide comprehensive information to Operations, enabling informed decision-making. If evidence is attached, it can include one or more items in various formats, such as bar or line charts, dynacards, images and even IFrames to external websites. === "View Full Details" You can also click on the popup icon { width=20 height=20 } to see the full details of the Recommendation. You can read about the Recommendation Popup in the [chapter below here](#asset-details-page).
### Asset Details Page When you select an Asset, in the Asset Details page under Applications tab you can view and filter all the Recommendations by status (Pending, Accepted, Rejected and Expired) for the selected Application. You can read more about this feature here. In the recommendation card you will see two types of options, both of which will open the standard Recommendation popup; Option | UI | Description --- | --- | --- **View More** Button | | This means that there is a pending Recommendation. Clicking will show a popup where you can accept/reject the pending Recommendation. **Details** Link | {height=25} | This means that there are NO pending Recommendations. Clicking will show a popup with the last Recommendation details. The Recommendation popup will appear. This shows you a Recommendation from the **View More** button where actions are still available.
=== "Details" In the Recommendations popup you can see the full details of the Recommendation on the left hand side. This will include information about; | Title | Description | |--------|--------------| | Recommendation Title | The name or title of the recommendation | | Current Status | The present state of the recommendation. | | Asset | Assets associated with the recommendation. Note that each associated action may not directly affect this Asset's Data Streams. | | Created By | What process created the Recommendation, usually an Kelvin SmartApp™. | | Description | Detailed explanation of the recommendation. | | Mode | Indicates if the recommendation is in Open or Closed Loop mode. | In the Advanced Info section; | Title | Description | |--------|--------------| | Created On | When the Recommendation was created. | | Expires On | Only visible when Recommendation is not yet Accepted/Rejected.

Date when the Recommendation will expire and be automatically Rejected. | | Accepted On | Only visible when Recommendation has been Accepted.

Date when the Recommendation was accepted, either automatically on Closed Loop or by Operations in Open Loop. | | Version | The Application version of the Kelvin SmartApp™ that created this Recommendation. | | ID | The unique ID for this Recommendation. This is useful for Developers for using Kelvin API to directly analyze the Recommendation's raw data programmatically. | === "Actions" The middle column named **Recommendation Actions** shows all the actions that will be taken if the recommendation is accepted. It is possible to have multiple actions in one Recommendation. This will also give you information about the current setting when the Recommendation was created as well as the Recommended new setting. The actions are not just limited to Control Changes but can also be one of the following; Even if a Recommendation is accepted, this does not mean the Actions will also succeed. It just means the Actions are initiated. There may be cases where a Recommendation is accepted and highlighted green, but the Control Change fails, for example due to Guardrail rule violation, and is highlighted Red. * Control Changes * Send Emails and Alerts (using Custom Actions) * Create Alarms (future feature) **Advanced Info** For each action, you can see advanced details on the action itself. Before accepting the Recommendation, you will see the action parameters that will be used when the Recommendation is Accepted. After accepting the Recommendation, you will also see the status of the action if it relevant, for example control changes. === "Evidences" Evidence are attachments to the recommendation that provide comprehensive information to Operations, enabling informed decision-making. If evidence is attached, it may include one or more items in various formats, such as bar or line charts, dynacards, images and even IFrames to external websites. === "Comments" You can add an unlimited amount of comments at any time to this Recommendation. You do not need to Accept or Reject a Recommendation to add a comment. Comments are very useful for; * Sharing information between Operations or shifts about certain Recommendations * As feedback to the Developer. The Developers can use the comments to determine how to improve the Kelvin SmartApps™ that produce the Recommendations. To see or add comments, click on the comments icon And then the comments will appear;
summary: Recommendations are generated from Kelvin SmartApp™ or from third party sources using the Kelvin API endpoint and will either be automatically accepted and implemented or wait for the Operations Engineers decision on the actions to take.
